title: Introduction to Quadratics
description: A first, GCSE-grade look at quadratic expressions: what makes an expression quadratic, expanding brackets to form one, factorising and solving simple cases, and the basic parabola shape.
objectives: Recognise a quadratic expression written as $ax^2+bx+c$ and identify $a$, $b$ and $c$
Expand a pair of brackets such as $(x+p)(x+q)$ to obtain a quadratic expression
Factorise simple quadratics $x^2+bx+c$ using integer factor pairs
Solve a quadratic equation in factorised form by setting each factor equal to zero
Describe the basic shape of a parabola and explain how the sign of $a$ decides whether it is u-shaped or n-shaped
Find where a parabola crosses the x-axis directly from its factorised form

lessons.title: What is a quadratic? Expanding brackets
